gloss.en: a short, catchy phrase used in ads or politics

words.example: The company chose a new advertising slogan.

gloss.en: a surface that is higher at one end

words.example: The town is built on a slope.

gloss.en: careless and untidy; not done well

words.example: His homework was full of sloppy mistakes.

gloss.en: a tall chimney on a factory or ship

words.example: Smoke poured from the factory smokestack.

gloss.en: to move quietly so others do not notice

words.example: She tried to sneak out of the house.

gloss.en: an unkind smile showing scorn

words.example: He answered with a cruel sneer.

gloss.en: to breathe in air through the nose noisily

words.example: The dog began to sniff the ground.

gloss.en: a loud breathing sound made while sleeping

words.example: His loud snore woke the whole family.

gloss.en: a ball of snow made by hand

words.example: The children threw a snowball at me.

gloss.en: called this name though it may not fit

words.example: This so-called expert knew nothing.

gloss.en: completely wet through

words.example: We got soaked in the heavy rain.

gloss.en: extremely wet; dripping with water

words.example: My clothes were soaking after the storm.

gloss.en: to cry loudly while breathing in short gasps

words.example: She began to sob when she heard the news.

gloss.en: friendly and enjoying being with people

words.example: He is a warm and sociable person.

gloss.en: to spend time with people in a friendly way

words.example: We often socialise with our neighbours.

gloss.en: to spend time with people in a friendly way

words.example: He likes to socialize with his coworkers.

gloss.en: the quality of being soft to touch

words.example: I love the softness of this blanket.

gloss.en: the top layer of earth where plants grow

words.example: This rich soil is good for farming.

gloss.en: of or connected with the sun

words.example: They put solar panels on the roof.

gloss.en: the quality of being firm and strong

words.example: The bridge is known for its solidity.

gloss.en: a speech an actor gives alone on stage

words.example: Hamlet's famous soliloquy moved the crowd.

gloss.en: a liquid that can dissolve other substances

words.example: Water is a common solvent for salt.

gloss.en: in a way that is not known or certain
gloss.zh: 以某种方式；不知怎么地

words.example: We must stop him somehow.

gloss.en: at a time not exactly known or decided

words.example: I saw him sometime last summer.

gloss.en: to some degree; a little

words.example: The result was somewhat different.

gloss.en: the husband of your daughter

words.example: Her son-in-law cooked dinner for everyone.

gloss.en: a poem with fourteen lines

words.example: He wrote a sonnet about love.

gloss.en: clever and complicated in how it works

words.example: They use highly sophisticated computer systems.

gloss.en: the spiritual part of a person

words.example: He believed his soul would live on.

gloss.en: the recorded music of a film

words.example: I love the soundtrack of this movie.

gloss.en: toward the south

words.example: The birds flew southward for the winter.

gloss.en: toward the south

words.example: The river flows southwards to the sea.

gloss.en: relating to the former Soviet Union

words.example: The book describes soviet history.

gloss.en: a bean used for food and oil

words.example: Farmers here grow soybean crops.

gloss.en: a place offering health and beauty treatments

words.example: We spent the weekend at a spa.

gloss.en: a pork rib with little meat on it

words.example: He ordered a plate of sparerib.

gloss.en: shining with small flashes of light

words.example: The lake looked sparkling in the sun.

gloss.en: a small common brown bird

words.example: A sparrow landed on the windowsill.

gloss.en: an expert in a particular area

words.example: She is a specialist in heart disease.

gloss.en: to become an expert in one area

words.example: Many students prefer not to specialize too soon.

gloss.en: something a person or place is known for

words.example: Fresh fish is this restaurant's specialty.

gloss.en: a group of similar animals or plants

words.example: This is a protected area for endangered species.

gloss.en: for one particular purpose or thing only

words.example: This book is written specifically for children.

gloss.en: to state something exactly and clearly

words.example: Remember to specify your size when ordering.

gloss.en: very impressive or striking

words.example: The coastal road has spectacular scenery.

gloss.en: a person watching an event or game

words.example: The stadium will hold 75,000 spectators.

gloss.en: to guess about something without full facts

words.example: We could only speculate about the reasons.

gloss.en: guessing about something without knowing the facts

words.example: His private life is the subject of much speculation.

gloss.en: how fast something moves or travels

words.example: The car drove at high speed.

gloss.en: a driver going faster than the limit

words.example: The police stopped a speeder on the highway.

gloss.en: holding your full attention; fascinated

words.example: The story held the children spellbound.

gloss.en: a strong-tasting seed or powder used in cooking

words.example: Ginger is a common spice in cooking.

gloss.en: to make liquid flow over a container's edge

words.example: Try not to spill the water on the floor.

gloss.en: connected with the spirit, not the body

words.example: She had a deep spiritual experience.

gloss.en: a wish to hurt or upset someone

words.example: He said it out of pure spite.

gloss.en: the sound or mark of liquid hitting something

words.example: The stone fell in with a loud splash.

gloss.en: a spreading mark of splashed liquid

words.example: There was a splatter of paint on the wall.

gloss.en: a disagreement that divides people

words.example: The band reformed years after their split.

gloss.en: to ruin or damage something good

words.example: Bad weather spoilt our camping trip.

gloss.en: a man who speaks for a group

words.example: A police spokesman answered the questions.

gloss.en: a person who speaks for a group

words.example: A council spokesperson made a statement.

gloss.en: a woman who speaks for a group

words.example: A government spokeswoman confirmed the news.

gloss.en: a company paying for an event or program

words.example: The show is funded by a corporate sponsor.

gloss.en: financial support given by a sponsor

words.example: They signed a large sponsorship deal.

gloss.en: connected with sports

words.example: The city hosted a major sporting event.

gloss.en: a small round mark of different colour

words.example: The leopard has dark spots on its fur.

gloss.en: tiny drops of liquid sent through the air

words.example: She used a spray to water the plants.

gloss.en: an increase in how far something reaches

words.example: They tried to stop the spread of the disease.

gloss.en: a computer program for tables of numbers

words.example: He kept the budget in a spreadsheet.

gloss.en: the season between winter and summer

words.example: Flowers begin to grow in spring.

gloss.en: a short, very fast run

words.example: He won the 100-metre sprint.

gloss.en: something that encourages action

words.example: The prize was a spur to work harder.

gloss.en: a fast racket sport played indoors

words.example: We play squash every weekend.

gloss.en: steady; not likely to change or fail

words.example: The patient's condition is now stable.

gloss.en: a neat pile of things on top of each other

words.example: There was a stack of books on the desk.

gloss.en: a step or period in development

words.example: I can't decide at this stage.

gloss.en: to walk unsteadily as if about to fall

words.example: He began to stagger after the long run.

gloss.en: a dirty mark that is hard to remove

words.example: There was a coffee stain on his shirt.

gloss.en: a small open-front table for selling things

words.example: He works on a market stall in town.

gloss.en: to speak with pauses and repeated sounds

words.example: He began to stammer when he felt nervous.

gloss.en: a public opinion or attitude on something

words.example: What is the newspaper's stance on the war?

gloss.en: a clear opinion or attitude on something

words.example: He took a firm stand on the issue.

gloss.en: normal or average; not special

words.example: This is the standard rate of tax.

gloss.en: kept ready for use if needed

words.example: We bought standby tickets for the flight.

gloss.en: a situation with no movement at all

words.example: Traffic came to a complete standstill.

gloss.en: a group of lines in a poem

words.example: The first stanza describes a winter morning.

gloss.en: to look at something for a long time

words.example: Everyone began to stare at me.

gloss.en: to surprise someone suddenly

words.example: The loud noise will startle the baby.

gloss.en: suffering or death from lack of food

words.example: Many people died of starvation that winter.

gloss.en: to suffer or die from lack of food

words.example: The animals were left to starve.

gloss.en: suffering badly from hunger; very hungry

words.example: After the hike I was absolutely starving.

gloss.en: provided or controlled by the government

words.example: She met with several state officials.

gloss.en: a wise and respected political leader

words.example: He was a respected world statesman.

gloss.en: happening across a whole state

words.example: They held a statewide election yesterday.

gloss.en: numbers that describe a set of facts

words.example: The statistics show a rise in sales.

gloss.en: the legal or social position of someone

words.example: They were granted refugee status.

gloss.en: gradually and in a regular way

words.example: The company's exports increased steadily.

gloss.en: developing gradually in a regular way

words.example: We've had five years of steady growth.

gloss.en: the hot gas water becomes when boiling

words.example: Steam rose from the boiling kettle.

gloss.en: a strong metal made of iron and carbon

words.example: The bridge is made of steel.

gloss.en: hard, strong, or determined like steel

words.example: She had a steely look of determination.

gloss.en: rising or falling sharply, not gradually

words.example: We climbed a steep hill.

gloss.en: one action in a series to reach a goal

words.example: We are taking steps to prevent pollution.

gloss.en: the man married to your mother, not your father

gloss.en: the woman married to your father, not your mother

gloss.en: serious and strict; not gentle

gloss.en: a dish of meat and vegetables cooked slowly

gloss.en: a thin piece of wood from a tree

gloss.en: covered with something that sticks to things

gloss.en: hard to bend or move

words.example: He cut a sheet of stiff cardboard.

gloss.en: a substance that makes you more active

words.example: Coffee is a popular stimulant.

gloss.en: to make something more active; to encourage

gloss.en: the act of encouraging activity or interest

gloss.en: unwilling to spend or give money

words.example: He was too stingy to buy a gift.

gloss.en: to have a strong, unpleasant smell

words.example: The old fish began to stink.
